heading: Civil action for death or personal injury in a place subject to exclusive jurisdiction of United States
label: 5001 Civil action for death or personal injury in a place subject to exclusive jurisdiction of United States
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)500116 U.S.C. 457, .Feb. 1, 1928, ch. 1545 Stat. 54In subsection (a), the words “civil action” are substituted for “action” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)500116 U.S.C. 457, .Feb. 1, 1928, ch. 1545 Stat. 54In subsection (a), the words “civil action” are substituted for “action” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.

children.heading: .—Death
children.label: a .—Death
children.text: In the case of the death of an individual by the neglect or wrongful act of another in a place subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the United States within a State, a right of action shall exist as though the place were under the jurisdiction of the State in which the place is located.

children.heading: .—Personal Injury
children.label: b .—Personal Injury
children.text: In a civil action brought to recover on account of an injury sustained in a place described in subsection (a), the rights of the parties shall be governed by the law of the State in which the place is located.
